title: Occupational Safety and Health at the Workplace
subtitle: A practical safety awareness course for reducing workplace risk and strengthening daily OSH habits.
whyThisMatters: Safety performance depends on what people notice, report, and correct before incidents happen. This course helps employees and supervisors understand workplace hazards, personal responsibility, and practical prevention routines in a way that supports safer operations.
builtFor: Employees in operational or site-based roles
Supervisors responsible for daily safety practices
Teams that need a shared OSH awareness baseline
walkAwayWith: Recognize common workplace hazards before they become incidents.
Apply safer work habits and basic control measures.
Understand personal responsibility in workplace safety and health.
Support reporting, housekeeping, and prevention routines.

modules.title: Safe Work Practices in Daily Operations

methodologyCaption: The workshop uses workplace examples, discussion, and practical hazard-recognition activities to make safety behavior concrete.
sourceDoc: course/documents/PDF/OCCUPATIONAL SAFETY AND HEALTH AT WORK PLACE.pdf
